Current Limitations **Critical Issue:** The analysis cannot be performed because: - No browser automation environment is available - Cannot load the actual webpage content - Cannot inspect DOM elements or image attributes - Cannot evaluate asynchronous content loading - Cannot assess visual context or image relevance ### 3. ```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait
from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
import time
import csv
from io import StringIO

# Set up browser
chrome_options = Options()
chrome_options.add_argument("--headless")
chrome_options.add_argument("--no-sandbox")
chrome_options.add_argument("--disable-dev-shm-usage")
chrome_options.add_argument("--disable-gpu")
chrome_options.add_argument("--window-size=1920,1080")
chrome_options.add_argument("--user-agent=Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36")

driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=chrome_options)

results = []

try:
    # Load the URL
    url = "https://www.bbc.com/culture/article/20251215-avatar-fire-and-ash-review"
    print(f"Loading URL: {url}")
    driver.get(url)
    
    # Wait for page to load completely
    time.sleep(5)
    
    # Wait for main content area
    try:
        WebDriverWait(driver, 15).until(
            EC.presence_of_element_located((By.TAG_NAME, "article"))
        )
    except:
        print("No article tag found, trying main tag")
        try:
            WebDriverWait(driver, 15).until(
                EC.presence_of_element_located((By.TAG_NAME, "main"))
            )
        except:
